rationale: This supervisor structure prioritizes safe, efficient exploration by preferring forward motion when clear, using single-action commit states to avoid oscillations, and handling obstacles with clear, non-oscillatory escape policies. Helper states (commit and scan) ensure atomic actions and recovery steps. All uncontrollable events are handled deterministically, and controllable actions are never enabled redundantly. The design avoids infinite loops and ensures systematic arena coverage.
strategy: Prefer forward movement in clear state for efficient coverage.
On front obstacle, perform a full scan to find a new direction (scan_full).
On left obstacle, rotate clockwise to escape; on right obstacle, rotate counterclockwise.
Commit states (forward_commit, rotate_commit_cw, rotate_commit_ccw) ensure atomic actions and immediate return to perception/decision states.
All uncontrollable events are handled deterministically in every state.
No oscillatory or redundant controllable actions are enabled in any state.
Systematic exploration is achieved by always attempting to move forward unless obstacles demand recovery.
Full scan is used as a recovery when blocked in front, helping to avoid deadlocks and improve coverage.
